laws.actName: State Emblem of India (Prohibition of Improper Use) Act, 2005
laws.title: Prohibition of improper use of emblem
laws.description: No person shall use the State Emblem of India or any imitation in a manner that creates an impression of association with the Government or as an official document without prior permission from the Central Government.
laws.simpleExplanation: Using the State Emblem in a way that makes people think it is official government work without permission is illegal.

punishments.description: Imprisonment up to two years, or fine up to 5000, or both.
For repeat offences, imprisonment not less than six months up to two years and fine up to 5000.

laws.actName: State Emblem of India (Prohibition of Improper Use) Act, 2005
laws.title: Prohibition of use of emblem for wrongful gain
laws.description: No person shall use the State Emblem of India for trade, business, profession, trademark, design, or patent except as prescribed.
laws.simpleExplanation: Using the State Emblem for business, trademarks, or profit without permission is illegal.

punishments.description: Imprisonment not less than six months up to two years and fine up to 5000 for wrongful gain.
